overview: Walls Combo is a five-color Pauper deck that leverages defender creatures and mana-producing barriers like Axebane Guardian and Overgrown Battlement. By pairing these with Freed From the Real or Galvanic Alchemist, the deck generates infinite mana to tutor for and execute various win conditions. It relies on card advantage engines and mana acceleration to assemble its combo quickly before opponents can interact.

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including basics for fixers), Quirion Ranger, Winding Way, or early wall/mana dorks.
Prioritize hands that can play a land and a Ranger/Winding Way by turn 2.
mulligan_criteria: hands with many doubles that don't do anything
cards you can't cast straight away
cards that cost different types of mana
Mulligan if you have 0-1 lands, no ramp/fixing, or only expensive spells without mana acceleration.

examples.reason: Potentially wins on turn 3 by accelerating Overgrown, enchanting it with Freed, and tutoring for Valakut Invoker.

early_game: Play mana dorks like Saruli Caretaker and Quirion Ranger to accelerate the curve.
Cast barriers with haste like Tuktuk Rubblefort and Crashing Drawbridge to enable early combo turns.
Turn 2: Cast Quirion Ranger or Winding Way to search a land and ramp.
If the opponent plays a creature, drop a wall to block.
Sequence lands to ensure colored mana for walls and ramp.
Use Ranger’s tap ability for flexible mana if needed for protection or combo pieces.
mid_game: Assemble the infinite mana loop using Axebane Guardian or Overgrown Battlement paired with Freed From the Real or Galvanic Alchemist.
Use tutors like Drift of Phantasms and Shield-Wall Sentinel to find missing combo pieces or win conditions.
Prioritize mana acceleration to cast combo pieces.
Use Quirion Ranger’s tap ability for flexible mana.
Don’t overcommit walls if you can win via combo.
Manage graveyard if relevant, and conserve cards in hand until you have a clear path to the win condition.
Commit walls only when necessary to survive until combo pieces are assembled.
Hold back high-value combo pieces until you have protection or a guaranteed win.
late_game: Execute win conditions such as Valakut Invoker, Vivien's Grizzly, or Secret Door using infinite mana.
Utilize Reaping the Graves to recover discarded combo pieces and create a secondary transmute loop.
The combo win condition, typically assembled through mana advantage and board stability.
Get there by ramping with Ranger/Winding Way and stabilizing with walls.
Once the combo is in hand or on the stack, execute it immediately if possible.
Go all-in when you have enough mana to cast your combo, have removed or blocked key threats, and can protect your win condition.
Commit when the opponent has no clear answer to your finisher or when you can trade your board for their life total.

key_cards.name: Axebane Guardian
key_cards.notes: Creates massive amounts of mana when enchanted or paired with barriers.

key_cards.name: Overgrown Battlement
key_cards.notes: Core barrier that generates mana; requires Orochi Leafcaller for color fixing if used.

key_cards.name: Freed From the Real
key_cards.notes: Enchantment that lets you untap the enchanted barrier to create more mana and repeat the loop.

key_cards.name: Galvanic Alchemist
key_cards.notes: Alternative to Freed that pairs with barriers to untap them and generate infinite mana.

key_cards.name: Drift of Phantasms
key_cards.notes: Transmutes to find key combo pieces or win conditions; hard to interact with due to flying.

key_cards.name: Valakut Invoker
key_cards.notes: Primary finisher found via tutors after generating infinite mana.

key_cards.name: Vivien's Grizzly
key_cards.notes: One-of finisher that draws your entire deck using infinite mana.

key_cards.name: Lead the Stampede
key_cards.notes: Main source of card advantage that searches for creatures to put into your hand.

key_cards.name: Winding Way
key_cards.notes: Main source of card advantage that searches for lands to put into your hand.

key_cards.name: Shield-Wall Sentinel
key_cards.notes: Adds consistency, upgrades barrier abilities, and acts as a tutor.

key_cards.name: Saruli Caretaker
key_cards.notes: Fixes mana and accelerates the curve with other creatures.

key_cards.name: Quirion Ranger
key_cards.notes: Works with Saruli to accelerate curve; also sideboarded to kill land destruction.

key_cards.name: Tuktuk Rubblefort
key_cards.notes: Gives haste to creatures to get the combo going early; has defender.

key_cards.name: Crashing Drawbridge
key_cards.notes: Gives haste to creatures; has defender.

key_cards.name: Tranquil Landscape
key_cards.notes: New land that adds consistency to the mana base and helps with splashes.

key_cards.name: Reaping the Graves
key_cards.notes: Gets back removed pieces, tutors via Drift, and enables a mini transmute loop.

key_cards.name: Orochi Leafcaller
key_cards.notes: Turns green mana into other colors to enable Overgrown Battlement combo.

sequencing_tips: Play Saruli Caretaker on turn 1 to generate initial mana.
Play Quirion Ranger on turn 2 to accelerate Overgrown Battlement and Orochi Leafcaller.
Enchant Overgrown Battlement with Freed From the Real on turn 3 to trigger infinite mana.
Tutor for and cast Valakut Invoker immediately after generating infinite mana.
Play cards in the exact order required to guarantee the combo on turn 3 if possible.
Common mistakes include overcommitting walls too early, mismanaging mana for combo, ignoring graveyard hate, and failing to track opponent’s win conditions.
Unique interactions: Quirion Ranger’s tap ability can be used for instant-speed mana for protection or combo pieces.
Winding Way’s extra land can enable combo turns.
Always play around sweepers and keep a backup plan if your combo is disrupted.

matchups.advice: Quirion Ranger effectively neutralizes land destruction; focus on developing your game plan without worrying about land drops.
Extremely unfavorable; sideboard helps but Krark-Clan Shaman and Galvanic Blast severely disrupt plans.
Slight advantage; exploit their slower start to combo quickly or bait removal with Reaping the Graves to create an opening.
Survive long enough by protecting cards and using Quirion Ranger with Gingerbread Cabin for life gain; aggro deck needs to close game quickly.
Race to inevitability; manage life against Cascade, play combo sequence carefully against Combo; side in Standard Bearer to disrupt Quirions.

Use cheap threats, split your combo into multiple steps, or use protection/disruption. Play around counters by having backup plans or casting at end step. If possible, use instant-speed interaction to clear the way for your combo.

Apply early pressure with walls, use removal on key ramp creatures, and race if your combo is faster. Disrupt their mana base if possible, and prioritize surviving until you can execute your own combo.
Stabilize with walls and cheap removal. Don’t overextend; focus on surviving until turn 4-5 to execute combo. Use life gain if available, and trade walls efficiently to preserve your combo pieces.

Pressure with early threats, use card advantage to out-value them, and protect your combo from sweepers. Play around their removal by having multiple threats or using protection. Force them to make difficult choices between your combo and board presence.

Win the resource war. Prioritize land drops, disrupt their ramp, and use targeted removal on their key combo pieces. Control the board with walls and force them to overcommit. Use hand disruption to steal key turns.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers, targeted removal, and combo win conditions.
Note if opponents are playing land destruction or discard.
Track graveyard hate if your combo relies on it, and monitor opponent’s mana development to predict their next play.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: This deck centers around defender creatures, and abuses Axebane Guardian 's and Overgrown Battlement 's abilities to create massive amounts of mana as fast as possible.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: your win condition, which is usually Valakut Invoker
venture into dungeons infinite times with Secret Door
put all your Generous Ent in play and move on to combat
Vivien's Grizzly is another excellent one-of in this deck that wins you the game

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Start on turn 1 with Saruli 's mana, then, on turn 2, put Quirion in play accelerating Overgrown and Orochi , and on turn 3 enchant Overgrown with Freed From the Real . That's how you get infinite mana to get your Valakut from your deck and win the game.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Krark-Clan Shaman simply destroys any of your hopes of following any of your plans, and Galvanic Blast kills any of your barriers.
Facing Lightning Bolt s and Chain Lightning s is quite sad
The recent addition of Refurbished Familiar gave this deck another powerful interaction through its bounce.
Standard Bearer is the most relevant card you can side in for these matchups as it disrupts Quirions and stops the combo.
